ActionItem.description: A single extracted action the principal should take.

due_hint.description: Free-text due hint as written ('Friday', 'EOD'); not parsed.
due_hint.title: Due Hint

type.description: Discriminator: 'text' for a plain imperative action; 'link' when the action involves following a URL (url is then required and non-empty).
type.title: Type

url.description: The URL to follow for a 'link' action item; None for 'text'.
url.title: Url

ActionItem.title: ActionItem

AttachmentMeta.description:
Metadata for one attachment on a message (schema 2.2, #1542).

Metadata only — no content. On the read path ``attachment_id`` is the
provider handle a caller uses to fetch the bytes (Gmail
``body.attachmentId``); it is null for providers/parts that carry none.

attachment_id.description: Provider handle for fetching the content (Gmail body.attachmentId). Null when the provider/part carries none.
attachment_id.title: Attachment Id

mime_type.description: MIME type as reported by the provider.
mime_type.title: Mime Type

AttachmentMeta.title: AttachmentMeta

BatchItemError.description:
Why one batch item could not be triaged (surfaced loudly, never swallowed).

Kept as an object (not a bare string) so a future ``code``/``type`` field is
additive rather than another breaking change.

message.description: Actionable failure reason for this item.
message.title: Message

BatchItemError.title: BatchItemError

BatchItemResult.description:
One item's outcome in a batch triage, correlated by 0-based index.

Exactly one of ``result`` or ``error`` is set. ``index`` matches the item's
position in the request ``items`` array so out-of-order processing is safe
(the current implementation is sequential and order-preserving).
BatchItemResult.title: BatchItemResult

BatchTriageRequest.description:
Top-level batch triage request envelope (#1887).

Accepts 1..MAX_BATCH_SIZE ``EmailInput`` items (same discriminated union as
the single-email endpoint's ``payload``). ``context`` applies to ALL items;
per-item context override is out of scope.

context.description: Optional context (people/projects/tone/self-email) applied to ALL items. Absent → behavior unchanged for every item.

items.description: The emails / threads to triage, 1..MAX_BATCH_SIZE items. Each is a SingleEmailInput or ThreadInput discriminated by ``kind``.
items.title: Items

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

BatchTriageRequest.title: BatchTriageRequest

BatchTriageResponse.description:
Top-level batch triage response envelope (#1887).

One ``BatchItemResult`` per request item, order-preserved, 1:1 with
``items``. HTTP 200 with all items errored is valid — consumers MUST inspect
each ``results[].error``, not just the HTTP status.

results.description: One entry per request item, order-preserved, 1:1 with ``items``.
results.title: Results

BatchTriageResponse.title: BatchTriageResponse

CalendarCreateEventRequest.description:
Create a calendar event. Shared by the preview (token-mint) and create
(token-consume) endpoints.

Creating an event is a Tier-2 (externally visible) mutation, so it is gated
by the same single-use confirmation-token handshake as ``/v1/email/send``:
POST this to ``/calendar/events/preview`` to mint a token bound to the exact
event, then echo the token in ``confirmation_token`` to ``/calendar/events``.

attendees.description: Attendee email addresses to invite (may be empty).
attendees.title: Attendees

confirmation_token.description: Confirmation token from POST /v1/email/calendar/events/preview. Ignored by preview; required by create — a create without a valid token bound to this exact event is rejected (403).
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

provider.description: Optional provider binding ('google' or 'microsoft'). When set on preview, the confirmation token binds to this provider so the create routes to the right calendar even with multiple accounts connected.
provider.title: Provider

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

CalendarCreateEventRequest.title: CalendarCreateEventRequest

CalendarEvent.description:
A calendar event as returned by the view endpoint (display-flattened).

``start`` / ``end`` are the raw provider strings (an RFC 3339 ``dateTime``
for timed events or a ``YYYY-MM-DD`` ``date`` for all-day events) — flattened
for display rather than re-modeled, matching what the agent's calendar tools
surface.

location.description: Free-text location, or null when none.
location.title: Location

organizer.description: Organizer email, or null when not reported.
organizer.title: Organizer

CalendarEvent.title: CalendarEvent

CalendarEventDateTime.description:
One endpoint (start or end) of a calendar event.

Provide EXACTLY ONE of:
  - ``date_time`` — a timed event, RFC 3339 (e.g. ``2026-07-01T14:00:00Z``
    or ``2026-07-01T14:00:00-07:00``); or
  - ``date`` — an all-day event, ``YYYY-MM-DD``.

``time_zone`` is an optional IANA name (e.g. ``America/Los_Angeles``).
Note: when ``time_zone`` is omitted on a timed event, the Microsoft Graph
(Outlook) calendar backend attaches a default of ``UTC`` (Graph rejects a
bare ``dateTime`` without a paired time zone). For Google, supply either a
UTC offset inside ``date_time`` or an explicit ``time_zone``.

date_time.description: Timed-event instant, RFC 3339. Mutually exclusive with 'date'.
date_time.title: Date Time

time_zone.description: IANA time zone (e.g. 'America/Los_Angeles'). Optional; the Outlook backend defaults a missing time zone to 'UTC' for timed events.
time_zone.title: Time Zone

CalendarEventDateTime.title: CalendarEventDateTime

CalendarEventPreviewResponse.description:
The normalized event echo plus a single-use confirmation token bound to
it. Returned by POST /v1/email/calendar/events/preview.

confirmation_token.description: Echo this back to POST /v1/email/calendar/events to authorize creating exactly this event. Single-use; bound to the event payload.
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

summary.description: The event title to be created.
summary.title: Summary

CalendarEventPreviewResponse.title: CalendarEventPreviewResponse

event_id.description: Provider id of the created event.
event_id.title: Event Id

events.description: Matching events, ordered by start time.
events.title: Events

CalendarRespondRequest.description:
RSVP to an existing calendar invite (POST /v1/email/calendar/events/respond).

Responding to an invite is an explicit, user-initiated action surfaced by the
UI's accept/decline controls, so it is not separately token-gated. ``status``
is the RSVP verb; ``attendee_email`` is the principal's own address (used by
the Google backend to locate the attendee row; ignored by the Outlook backend,
which RSVPs on the authenticated ``/me`` calendar).

attendee_email.description: The principal's own email (the attendee responding). Used by the Google backend; ignored by Outlook (RSVPs on /me).
attendee_email.title: Attendee Email

event_id.description: Provider event id to RSVP to.
event_id.title: Event Id

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

status.description: RSVP response: accept, decline, or tentatively accept.
status.title: Status

CalendarRespondRequest.title: CalendarRespondRequest

event_id.description: The event that was responded to.
event_id.title: Event Id

status.description: The RSVP response that was recorded.
status.title: Status

DraftReply.description:
A drafted reply the agent proposes. Never sent without confirmation
(#1264) — this is a proposal, not an action.

attachments.description: Metadata of the attachments the draft proposes to send (schema 2.2, #1542). Content stays in the caller's OutgoingAttachment payload; only the echo travels here.
attachments.title: Attachments

DraftReply.title: DraftReply

DraftScaffold.description:
A reply *scaffold* the triage path proposes — recipient + subject only,
no body (schema 2.3).

Triage classifies and summarizes; it never composes reply prose. So the
scaffold it returns carries only the resolved reply recipient and a
``Re:``-prefixed subject. To turn it into a sendable reply, compose the body
yourself (e.g. an LLM call over the triage ``summary`` + ``action_items`` +
the original message) and pass ``(to, subject, body)`` to
``POST /v1/email/draft`` — that endpoint returns a full :class:`DraftReply`
and a single-use send-confirmation token.
DraftScaffold.title: DraftScaffold

EmailActionConfirmRequest.description:
Request a single-use confirmation token for a destructive mailbox action.

Mirrors the draft→send handshake (#1264): nothing mutates here — this only
mints a token bound to *this* ``(action, message_id)`` that the matching
``/archive`` or ``/quarantine`` call must echo back. A token minted for one
action/message cannot authorize a different one.

message_id.description: Provider message id the action will mutate.
message_id.title: Message Id

provider.description: Optional provider binding ('google' / 'microsoft'). When set, the minted token is bound to this mailbox so the action routes correctly even when more than one mailbox is connected.
provider.title: Provider

EmailActionConfirmRequest.title: EmailActionConfirmRequest

EmailActionConfirmResponse.description: A single-use confirmation token bound to the requested action+message.

confirmation_token.description: Echo to POST /v1/email/archive or /v1/email/quarantine to authorize exactly this (action, message_id). Single-use; bound to the action.
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

EmailActionConfirmResponse.title: EmailActionConfirmResponse

EmailAddress.description:
A single email participant. ``name`` is optional (many headers carry a
bare address); ``email`` is required and must look like an address.
EmailAddress.title: EmailAddress

EmailArchiveRequest.description:
Archive a message (remove it from the inbox). Requires a confirmation
token minted by POST /v1/email/confirm for ``action='archive'``.

confirmation_token.description: Token from POST /v1/email/confirm. An archive without a valid token for this exact (action='archive', message_id) is rejected (403).
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

provider.description: Optional provider ('google' / 'microsoft'), used only when the token carries no binding. A token's bound provider always wins; with two mailboxes connected and neither set, the call is rejected (400).
provider.title: Provider

EmailArchiveRequest.title: EmailArchiveRequest

EmailArchiveResponse.description: Result of an archive — carries the undo handle for the 30s window.

batch_id.description: Undo handle: pass to POST /v1/email/unarchive within the undo window to restore the message to the inbox.
batch_id.title: Batch Id

post_archive_id.description: The message id valid AFTER the archive. For folder-based backends (Outlook) the archive move mints a new id; for Gmail it equals the request id. Surfaced so a caller can track the message post-archive.
post_archive_id.title: Post Archive Id

undo_window_seconds.description: Seconds the unarchive handle stays valid.
undo_window_seconds.title: Undo Window Seconds

EmailArchiveResponse.title: EmailArchiveResponse

EmailBriefingResponse.description:
Latest scheduled inbox briefing (#1608). LOCAL model — the briefing
payload itself is the frozen contract's ``EmailPreScanResult``.

briefing.description: The email_pre_scan envelope the scheduled run produced.

generated_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp of the scheduled run.
generated_at.title: Generated At

EmailBriefingResponse.title: EmailBriefingResponse

EmailCategory.description:
The five-bucket triage taxonomy (schema 2.0, #1615). Values MUST match
``triage_heuristics.ALL_CATEGORIES`` — the contract tests assert this.
EmailCategory.title: EmailCategory

EmailDraftRequest.description: Propose a reply and obtain a confirmation token for it.

attachments.description: Attachments to include on the send (schema 2.2, #1542). The minted confirmation token binds to their content digests, so the send must carry these exact files.
attachments.title: Attachments

provider.description: Optional provider binding ('google' or 'microsoft'). When set, the confirmation token is bound to this provider so the send routes to the correct mailbox even when multiple are connected.
provider.title: Provider

EmailDraftRequest.title: EmailDraftRequest

EmailDraftResponse.description: The proposed reply plus a single-use confirmation token bound to it.

confirmation_token.description: Echo this back to POST /v1/email/send to authorize sending exactly this payload. Single-use; bound to (to, subject, body, attachments).
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

EmailDraftResponse.title: EmailDraftResponse

EmailMessage.description: One email message. Shared by the single-email and thread inputs.

attachments.description: Attachment metadata on this message (schema 2.2, #1542). Metadata only — triage weighs names/types/sizes; content never travels here.
attachments.title: Attachments

from.description: Sender. Aliased to 'from' (a Python keyword) on the wire.

thread_id.description: Provider thread id this message belongs to.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

EmailMessage.title: EmailMessage

EmailPreScanRequest.description: Request envelope for an inbox pre-scan (#1778). Read-only.

max_messages.description: How many recent inbox messages to scan. Bounded so a caller can't request an unbounded mailbox sweep.
max_messages.title: Max Messages

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

EmailPreScanRequest.title: EmailPreScanRequest

EmailPreScanResult.description:
The aggregate pre-scan envelope the ``EmailPreScanCard`` renders.

This is the exact payload the chat surface detects via
``kind == "email_pre_scan"`` — produced by the agent's
``pre_scan_inbox_impl`` (same classification path), not re-implemented here.

informational_count.description: Count of informational (FYI/PERSONAL) messages — not listed.
informational_count.title: Informational Count

kind.description: Discriminator the chat surface detects to render the card.
kind.title: Kind

preferences_applied.description: Session preferences that shaped this pre-scan.

suggested_archives.description: Promotional / low-priority messages suggested for archive (capped).
suggested_archives.title: Suggested Archives

suggested_drafts.description: Reserved for future LLM-driven draft generation; empty today.
suggested_drafts.title: Suggested Drafts

EmailPreScanResult.title: EmailPreScanResult

EmailQuarantineRequest.description:
Quarantine a phishing message (apply GAIA_PHISHING_QUARANTINE + archive).
Requires a confirmation token for ``action='quarantine'``.

Gmail-only: the label-based quarantine and its reversible undo don't map onto
Outlook's folder moves, so a request that resolves to an Outlook mailbox is
rejected with 400 rather than performing a move undo can't reverse (#1738).

confirmation_token.description: Token from POST /v1/email/confirm. A quarantine without a valid token for this exact (action='quarantine', message_id) is rejected (403).
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

is_phishing.description: Must be true. The action refuses to quarantine a message not flagged as phishing — a safety gate, never silently bypassed.
is_phishing.title: Is Phishing

provider.description: Optional provider ('google' / 'microsoft'), used only when the token carries no binding (see EmailArchiveRequest.provider).
provider.title: Provider

EmailQuarantineRequest.title: EmailQuarantineRequest

EmailQuarantineResponse.description: Result of a quarantine — carries the action id for the 30s undo.

action_id.description: Undo handle: pass to POST /v1/email/unquarantine within the undo window to restore the message's prior labels.
action_id.title: Action Id

prior_labels.description: The label set restored on undo (recorded pre-quarantine).
prior_labels.title: Prior Labels

quarantine_label_id.description: Id of the GAIA_PHISHING_QUARANTINE label that was applied.
quarantine_label_id.title: Quarantine Label Id

undo_window_seconds.description: Seconds the unquarantine handle stays valid.
undo_window_seconds.title: Undo Window Seconds

EmailQuarantineResponse.title: EmailQuarantineResponse

EmailSearchRequest.description:
Search the connected mailbox for messages by Gmail-style query / labels.

Read-only: this lists messages already in the mailbox; it never sends,
modifies, or triages. It restores the agent's in-loop inbox-search tool
(``search_messages``) on the REST contract so the Agent UI can drive it
through the package (#1781).

Both ``query`` and ``labels`` are optional. A ``query`` searches **all
mail** (Gmail search semantics); ``labels`` filter to those labels; with
**neither**, the search lists the INBOX (bounded by ``max_results``).

labels.description: Label ids to filter by (e.g. ['INBOX', 'UNREAD']). Omit to apply no label filter; when query is also omitted, the search defaults to the INBOX.
labels.title: Labels

max_results.description: Max messages to return (1-100). Each match is hydrated with a per-message fetch, so the count is capped to bound that fan-out.
max_results.title: Max Results

page_token.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's ``next_page_token``. None fetches the first page.
page_token.title: Page Token

query.description: Gmail-style search query (e.g. 'from:alice is:unread'). A query searches all mail; omit it to list by label (or the INBOX when labels are also omitted).
query.title: Query

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

EmailSearchRequest.title: EmailSearchRequest

next_page_token.description: Opaque token to fetch the next page, or null when no more.
next_page_token.title: Next Page Token

query.description: Echoes the request query (None when unset).
query.title: Query

EmailSearchResultItem.description:
One message in a search result — inbox-list metadata, not the full body.

Header values (``from_``/``to``/``date``) are the raw, provider-decoded
header strings exactly as the agent's read tools surface them — not parsed
into ``EmailAddress`` objects, because list headers can carry multiple or
malformed addresses a strict parse would reject. Fetch the full message via
the triage path when you need the body.

from.description: Raw 'From' header string. Aliased to 'from' on the wire.
from.title: From

snippet.description: Provider-supplied short preview of the body.
snippet.title: Snippet

thread_id.description: Provider thread id this message belongs to.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

EmailSearchResultItem.title: EmailSearchResultItem

EmailSendRequest.description: Send a reply. Requires a valid confirmation token for this payload.

attachments.description: Attachments to send (schema 2.2, #1542). Must match the set the confirmation token was minted for — filename, MIME type, and content digest all bind.
attachments.title: Attachments

confirmation_token.description: Confirmation token from POST /v1/email/draft. A send without a valid token for this exact payload is rejected (403).
confirmation_token.title: Confirmation Token

provider.description: Optional provider ('google' or 'microsoft'), used ONLY as the fallback when the confirmation token carries no provider binding. A token's bound provider always wins; with two mailboxes connected and neither a binding nor this field set, the send is rejected as ambiguous (400).
provider.title: Provider

EmailSendRequest.title: EmailSendRequest

attachments.description: Metadata of the attachments that went out (schema 2.2).
attachments.title: Attachments

sent_id.description: Provider message id of the sent email.
sent_id.title: Sent Id

to.description: Recipients the message was sent to.
to.title: To

EmailTriageRequest.description: Top-level request envelope shared by REST (#1229) and MCP stdio (#1104).

context.description: Optional context (people/projects/tone/self-email) that biases categorization and summary. Absent → behavior unchanged.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Mismatch lets a consumer fail loudly.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

EmailTriageRequest.title: EmailTriageRequest

EmailTriageResponse.description: Top-level response envelope shared by REST (#1229) and MCP stdio (#1104).

request_kind.description: Which input shape produced this result.
request_kind.title: Request Kind

EmailTriageResponse.title: EmailTriageResponse

EmailTriageResult.description: The structured analysis of a single email or thread.

attachments.description: Attachment metadata of the analyzed message (or every message in the thread, oldest-first), echoed for downstream processing (schema 2.2, #1542).
attachments.title: Attachments

category.description: One of the five taxonomy buckets (schema 2.0).

draft.description: Proposed reply SCAFFOLD (recipient + subject only, no body), or null when no reply is suggested (schema 2.3). Triage never composes reply prose; to send a reply, compose the body and POST it to /v1/email/draft.

message_id.description: Echoes the provider message-id from the request (SingleEmailInput.message or ThreadInput.thread_id). Null when the result was produced from a raw Gmail-API message (no contract message_id available).
message_id.title: Message Id

suggested_action.description: Suggested next action: reply (URGENT/NEEDS_RESPONSE), archive (PROMOTIONAL), or none (FYI/PERSONAL). Derived by precedence rule -- never required so existing consumers are unaffected.
suggested_action.title: Suggested Action

usage.description: LLM usage metrics (tokens + aggregate TPS) for this triage. Null on the heuristic-only path where no LLM call was made. Compare prompt_tokens against the agent's context-window envelope (16K target / 32K max, #1892) to see how much of the window a payload consumed.

EmailTriageResult.title: EmailTriageResult

EmailUnarchiveRequest.description: Reverse an archive within the undo window. NOT gated — it restores.

batch_id.description: The undo handle returned by POST /v1/email/archive.
batch_id.title: Batch Id

provider.description: Optional provider ('google' / 'microsoft'). Omit to route by the mailbox recorded at archive time (the default and correct choice).
provider.title: Provider

EmailUnarchiveRequest.title: EmailUnarchiveRequest

EmailUnarchiveResponse.description: Result of an unarchive — partial success is reported, never silent.

batch_id.description: The undo handle that was processed.
batch_id.title: Batch Id

failed.description: Messages that could not be restored (with reasons).
failed.title: Failed

restored.description: Count of messages restored to inbox.
restored.title: Restored

undone.description: True when at least one restored.
undone.title: Undone

EmailUnarchiveResponse.title: EmailUnarchiveResponse

EmailUnquarantineRequest.description: Reverse a quarantine within the undo window. NOT gated — it restores.

action_id.description: The action id returned by POST /v1/email/quarantine.
action_id.title: Action Id

provider.description: Optional provider ('google' / 'microsoft'); omit to route by the mailbox recorded at quarantine time.
provider.title: Provider

EmailUnquarantineRequest.title: EmailUnquarantineRequest

action_id.description: The action id that was undone.
action_id.title: Action Id

HealthResponse.description:
Liveness/readiness probe for the email surface.

Dependency-light by design: it never touches a live mailbox or the LLM, so a
host can confirm the router is mounted and serving before any connector or
model is configured.

status.description: Always 'ok' when the surface is serving.
status.title: Status

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse

InitLemonadeStatus.description: Reachability AND version-compatibility of the local Lemonade Server.

base_url.description: The /api/v1 base URL that was probed.
base_url.title: Base Url

compatible.description: True when version >= min_version. Null when the version could not be determined (the check was indeterminate, not a pass).
compatible.title: Compatible

min_version.description: Minimum Lemonade version the triage stack requires (gaia_agent_email.version.MIN_LEMONADE_VERSION).
min_version.title: Min Version

reachable.description: True when Lemonade answered the /health probe.
reachable.title: Reachable

version.description: Lemonade's self-reported server version (from /health). Null when the server doesn't advertise one.
version.title: Version

InitLemonadeStatus.title: InitLemonadeStatus

InitModelStatus.description: Presence (and, when cheap, loadability) of the triage model.

ctx_size.description: The context window the model is CURRENTLY loaded at, as reported by the server's /health (recipe_options.ctx_size). Null whenever the model is not loaded right now or the server does not report a ctx — never a config echo or a guess (#1892). Compare against the envelope (16384 target / 32768 max) to see what window a run would actually measure.
ctx_size.title: Ctx Size

id.description: Resolved Lemonade model id for triage.
id.title: Id

loadable.description: Whether the model actually loads. Not probed in v1 (forcing a load is heavy), so this is null — `present` is the readiness signal. Reserved for an opt-in deeper check.
loadable.title: Loadable

present.description: True when the model is downloaded on the server.
present.title: Present

InitModelStatus.title: InitModelStatus

InitResponse.description:
Readiness preflight for the whole triage stack (#1795).

``ready`` is True only when Lemonade is reachable AND the triage model is
present. The route returns HTTP 200 when ready and 503 when not, with an
actionable ``hint`` naming what to fix — so an integrator can verify "ready
to triage," not just "process up." Read-only: probes only, no model pull.

hint.description: Actionable next step when not ready (what failed / what to do). Null when ready.
hint.title: Hint

ready.description: True when the triage stack is ready to serve.
ready.title: Ready

InitResponse.title: InitResponse

OutgoingAttachment.description:
One attachment to include on a draft/send (schema 2.2, #1542).

Content travels inline as standard (RFC 4648) base64. Validation is
fail-loud: a non-decodable payload, a malformed MIME type, or a decoded
size over ``MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES`` is a ``ValidationError`` — never
silently truncated or dropped.

mime_type.description: MIME type of the content, e.g. 'application/pdf'.
mime_type.title: Mime Type

OutgoingAttachment.title: OutgoingAttachment

PreScanItem.description:
One surfaced inbox message in a pre-scan section.

``why`` carries the rationale for urgent/actionable rows; ``reason`` carries
it for suggested-archive rows. The frontend card reads ``reason ?? why``, so
exactly one is populated per row depending on the section.

reason.description: Rationale for a suggested-archive row (the heuristic reason).
reason.title: Reason

sender.description: Raw 'From' header of the message (display + address).
sender.title: Sender

thread_id.description: Provider thread id this message belongs to.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

why.description: Rationale for an urgent/actionable row (the heuristic reason).
why.title: Why

PreScanItem.title: PreScanItem

PreScanPreferencesApplied.description:
The session sender/category preferences that shaped this pre-scan.

Always present (with empty defaults) so the frontend schema can lock in now;
the stateless REST surface leaves these empty today, but the field carries a
stable shape for a future preference-aware caller.
PreScanPreferencesApplied.title: PreScanPreferencesApplied

PreScanTotals.description:
Pre-cap counts per bucket — the true totals before the per-section caps
are applied to the surfaced lists. Lets the card show "N surfaced" honestly.
PreScanTotals.title: PreScanTotals

cancelled.description: True once the cancel was delivered to the run.
cancelled.title: Cancelled

run_id.description: The run that was signalled to cancel.
run_id.title: Run Id

QueryContextItem.description: One prior turn pushed in the request body (spec §2.4).

content.description: The message text for this turn.
content.title: Content

QueryContextItem.title: QueryContextItem

context.description: The relevant transcript slice, pushed in the body. May be an empty array for a fresh conversation, but the field must be present.
context.title: Context

max_steps.description: Agent-loop step ceiling. Omitted → the agent's configured default.
max_steps.title: Max Steps

model.description: Model id override. Omitted → the sidecar's default.
model.title: Model

provider.description: LLM provider override. The email agent runs local inference only, so only 'lemonade' is accepted; any other value is rejected (400).
provider.title: Provider

query.description: The natural-language request driving the agent loop.
query.title: Query

run_id.description: Host-minted streaming-run handle (UUIDv4). Cancellation (POST /v1/email/query/{run_id}/cancel) keys off it, so the run is cancellable from the instant the request is sent.
run_id.title: Run Id

SingleEmailInput.description: A single email to triage (AC3 — single-email path).

principal.description: The recipient the agent acts on behalf of — whose inbox this is. Distinct from per-message 'to': the principal is the account owner, not necessarily a recipient of every message in a thread.

SingleEmailInput.title: SingleEmailInput

ThreadInput.description: A full conversation thread to triage (AC3 — thread path).

messages.description: Every message in the thread, oldest-first. Non-empty.
messages.title: Messages

principal.description: The recipient the agent acts on behalf of — whose inbox this is. Distinct from per-message 'to': the principal is the account owner, not necessarily a recipient of every message in a thread.

thread_id.description: Provider thread id for the conversation.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

ThreadInput.title: ThreadInput

TriageContext.description:
Optional caller-supplied context that biases categorization/summary
(#1541). Absent → behavior is identical to today. Coexists with gaia
memory (#1114) without requiring it.

people.description: Important people whose mail should weigh higher.
people.title: People

projects.description: Active projects the principal cares about.
projects.title: Projects

self_email.description: The principal's own address, so the model knows who 'I' is.
self_email.title: Self Email

TriageContext.title: TriageContext

TriageUsage.description:
LLM usage metrics for a triage, aggregated across the classify +
summarize calls. Reuses the existing ``AgentResponse.stats`` measurement
(#1277/#1278) — no new measurement path. ``None`` on the heuristic-only
path where no LLM call is made.

completion_tokens.description: Sum of output (completion) tokens across the LLM calls.
completion_tokens.title: Completion Tokens

prompt_tokens.description: Sum of input tokens across the LLM calls.
prompt_tokens.title: Prompt Tokens

tokens_per_second.description: Aggregate decode throughput (total output tokens / total decode time).
tokens_per_second.title: Tokens Per Second

total_tokens.description: Sum of input + output tokens across the LLM calls.
total_tokens.title: Total Tokens

TriageUsage.title: TriageUsage

UnarchiveFailure.description: One message in the batch that failed to restore.

message_id.description: The message that failed to restore.
message_id.title: Message Id

UnarchiveFailure.title: UnarchiveFailure

UnarchivedMessage.description: One message restored to the inbox by an unarchive.
UnarchivedMessage.title: UnarchivedMessage

VersionResponse.description:
The two version numbers a host negotiates against.

``apiVersion`` is the frozen REST/contract version (a contract bump bumps it);
``agentVersion`` is the package build. Both come from
``gaia_agent_email.version`` so this endpoint and the freeze server's
``/version`` report identical values.
VersionResponse.title: VersionResponse

info.description: REST surface for the GAIA Email Triage agent (/v1/email/*). Cross-implementation contract for the npm client and native builds.
info.title: GAIA Email Agent API

post.description:
Archive a message — gated on confirmation, reversible for 30s.

The gate fires FIRST: a request without a valid token for this exact
``(action='archive', message_id)`` is rejected with 403 before any backend
call. On success a ``batch_id`` undo handle is returned; pass it to
POST /v1/email/unarchive within the window to restore the message. The
response also surfaces ``post_archive_id`` (the id a folder backend mints on
the move) so a caller can track the message after Outlook changes its id.

400.description: Ambiguous or unknown account: the named provider is not connected, or no provider was given while several accounts are connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

get.description:
Return the latest scheduled inbox briefing (#1608).

The briefing is generated without a user prompt by the sidecar's daily
scheduler (off by default — ``GAIA_EMAIL_BRIEFING_ENABLED``) and
persisted by ``gaia_agent_email.briefing``; this endpoint is the pull
surface any host reads it from. 404 until a scheduled run has happened.

404.description: No briefing generated yet — enable the daily schedule (GAIA_EMAIL_BRIEFING_ENABLED) or POST /v1/email/prescan.

500.description: The persisted briefing could not be read or does not match the email_pre_scan envelope.

get.description:
View calendar events on the primary calendar (read-only).

``time_min`` / ``time_max`` are optional RFC 3339 bounds. ``provider``
(google|microsoft) is required only when more than one account is connected;
with exactly one, it is inferred. Reaches whichever calendar the user
connected. Fails loudly (403 + reconnect CTA) if the calendar scope is missing.

400.description: Ambiguous or unknown account: the named provider is not connected, or no provider was given while several accounts are connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Create a calendar event — gated on explicit confirmation (#1780).

Mirrors the send gate: a request without a valid, payload-bound confirmation
token (from POST /calendar/events/preview) is rejected with HTTP 403 before
any backend call. Creating an event is externally visible to attendees, so it
is never performed without explicit confirmation.

400.description: Bad event input (no usable time / inverted window), or the provider is ambiguous/not connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Mint a single-use confirmation token bound to the proposed event.

The calendar analogue of POST /v1/email/draft: it creates nothing and reads
no calendar — it only returns the normalized event plus a ``confirmation_token``
the caller echoes to POST /v1/email/calendar/events to authorize the create.

RSVP accept / decline / tentative to an existing calendar invite.

An explicit, user-initiated action (the UI's accept/decline controls), so it
is not separately token-gated. ``attendee_email`` is the principal's own
address (used by the Google backend; ignored by Outlook, which RSVPs on /me).

400.description: Ambiguous or unknown account: the named provider is not connected, or no provider was given while several accounts are connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Mint a single-use confirmation token for a destructive mailbox action.

The token authorizes exactly one ``(action, message_id)`` — the archive or
quarantine call must echo it back. Nothing mutates here; this is the
explicit user-confirmation step (the action analogue of POST /v1/email/draft
for sends). When ``provider`` is set the token carries that binding so the
action routes to the right mailbox.

Propose a reply and mint a confirmation token bound to its payload.

The returned token must be echoed to :func:`send_email` to authorize
sending exactly this ``(to, subject, body, attachments)``. This is the explicit
user-confirmation step — the consuming app surfaces the draft to the
user, and only a user-approved send echoes the token back.

get.description:
Report that the email REST surface is mounted and serving.

Dependency-light: no live mailbox, no LLM. A host uses this for the sidecar
readiness handshake and for liveness checks once mounted on the product app.

Readiness preflight: validate the whole triage stack (#1795).

Returns HTTP 200 when ready, 503 when not (with an actionable ``hint``).
Unlike ``/health`` (liveness-only — never touches the LLM), this probes the
local Lemonade Server, checks it is at a compatible VERSION (>= the agent's
``MIN_LEMONADE_VERSION``), and confirms the triage model is downloaded — so a
host can verify "ready to triage," not just "process up." Read-only — no
model pull or provisioning is triggered.

post.description:
Pre-scan the connected inbox into the scannable triage card envelope.

Lists the ``max_messages`` most-recent inbox messages via every connected
mailbox and returns the aggregate pre-scan summary the Agent UI's
``EmailPreScanCard`` renders — top urgent / actionable rows, an
informational count, and suggested archives, each with a heuristic reason.
Read-only: nothing is archived, marked, or sent.

Classification reuses the agent's ``pre_scan_inbox_impl`` (the same
heuristic path the agent loop runs) — categories are not re-implemented
here. The backend is resolved by :func:`get_prescan_backend`: 503 when no
mailbox is connected, and with 2+ connected it consolidates every mailbox
into one merged envelope rather than failing on ambiguity (the frozen REST
contract carries no per-item mailbox tag).

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Quarantine a phishing message — gated on confirmation, reversible for 30s.

Applies the ``GAIA_PHISHING_QUARANTINE`` label and removes the message from
the inbox (capability #9). The gate fires FIRST (403 without a valid token
for this ``(action='quarantine', message_id)``). The action refuses
``is_phishing=False`` (400) — only phishing-flagged mail may be quarantined.
Reverse with POST /v1/email/unquarantine using the returned ``action_id``.

400.description: Refused: is_phishing was false (only phishing-flagged mail may be quarantined), the mailbox is Outlook (quarantine is Gmail-only), or the provider is ambiguous/not connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Run the email agent loop for one request and stream canonical SSE events.

Builds an agent, injects the pushed ``context`` as conversation history, runs
``process_query(query)`` on a worker thread with an ``SSEOutputHandler``, and
relays the loop as the seven canonical event types (spec §4). The stream ends
with exactly one ``final`` or ``error``. A confirmation-requiring step ends the
stream with a ``needs_confirmation`` followed by a ``final`` refusal (the
stateless D1 stub — see module docstring).

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream (text/event-stream). Each `data:` line is one canonical event discriminated on `type`, one of: status {message} | token {delta} | tool_call {tool, args} | tool_result {tool, render?, data} | needs_confirmation {run_id, action, summary} | final {answer, usage?} | error {detail, status}. The stream is terminated by exactly one `final` or one `error`.

post.description:
Cancel an in-flight ``/query`` run — stops tool execution between steps.

Cooperative, not a kill: it sets the run's cancel flag, which the agent loop
observes at its next step boundary (per-tool timeouts keep each step bounded,
so that point is always reached in finite time) and the handler observes while
waiting on any confirmation. 404 if no run with that id is in flight.

Search the connected mailbox (read-only) — #1781.

Lists messages matching ``query``/``labels`` from the connected Gmail or
Outlook mailbox and returns inbox-list metadata (id, thread, subject, from,
to, date, snippet, labels) — not the message body. No mail is sent or
modified. This restores the agent's in-loop inbox-search capability on the
REST contract so the Agent UI can drive it through the package.

The mailbox is resolved by :func:`get_search_backend` (fail-loud on 0 or
ambiguous mailbox counts). Backend auth / config / transport errors surface
as actionable 4xx/5xx, never a silent empty result.

400.description: Ambiguous or unknown account: the named provider is not connected, or no provider was given while several accounts are connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Send a reply — gated on explicit confirmation (#1264).

The confirmation gate is enforced FIRST: a request without a valid,
payload-bound confirmation token is rejected with HTTP 403 before any
backend call (or even backend resolution). This mirrors the agent's
``CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED_TOOLS`` guard, translated to the API boundary,
and guarantees the gate fires regardless of backend health. Never
auto-confirms.

400.description: Ambiguous or unknown account: the named provider is not connected, or no provider was given while several accounts are connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

413.description: An attachment exceeds the connected provider's size ceiling.

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Triage a single email or a full thread.

Accepts the FROZEN #1262 ``EmailTriageRequest`` (a single-email or
thread payload, discriminated on ``kind``) and returns the structured
``EmailTriageResponse`` — category, spam/phishing signals, a plain-text
summary, extracted action items, and an optional draft-reply proposal.
No mail is read or sent; this analyzes only the payload in the request.
Extracted action items are also persisted to the local task store,
linked to the source ``message_id`` and de-duplicated per message
(#1605) — additive, the response shape is unchanged.

502.description: Local LLM triage failed — Lemonade Server unreachable or it returned an unusable result.

post.description:
Triage an array of emails or threads in a single request (#1887).

Accepts a ``BatchTriageRequest`` (1..MAX_BATCH_SIZE ``EmailInput`` items)
and returns a ``BatchTriageResponse`` — one ``BatchItemResult`` per item,
order-preserved. Per-item failures surface as ``BatchItemResult.error``
(HTTP 200 with all items errored is valid — inspect each result). A 502
means Lemonade was unreachable or the triage model is unavailable there,
detected before any item was processed. No mail is read or sent; this
analyzes only the items in the request. Each
successful item's action items are persisted to the local task store,
linked to its source ``message_id`` and de-duplicated per message
(#1605) — additive, the response shape is unchanged.

502.description: Local LLM triage failed — Lemonade Server unreachable or it returned an unusable result.

post.description:
Reverse an archive within the undo window (NOT gated — it restores).

Routes each row to the mailbox it was archived from and uses the recorded
``post_archive_id`` so a folder-based backend (Outlook) can find the message
after its id changed (#1738). Fails loudly (409) when the window has expired
or the ``batch_id`` has no undoable rows — never a silent no-op.

400.description: Ambiguous or unknown account: the named provider is not connected, or no provider was given while several accounts are connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

409.description: The undo window has expired, or the handle is unknown or already undone — the action can no longer be reversed via the API.

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

post.description:
Reverse a quarantine within the undo window (NOT gated — it restores).

Restores the exact label set recorded at quarantine time and removes the
quarantine label. Fails loudly (409) when the window has expired or the
``action_id`` is unknown/already undone — never a silent no-op.

400.description: Refused: the recorded mailbox is Outlook (quarantine is Gmail-only), or the provider is ambiguous/not connected.

403.description: Authorization failed — the account's auth is missing, expired, or revoked (reconnect via Settings → Connectors), or a confirmation gate rejected the request (mint a fresh confirmation token).

409.description: The undo window has expired, or the handle is unknown or already undone — the action can no longer be reversed via the API.

503.description: No account connected, or backend configuration is missing — connect Google or Microsoft in Settings → Connectors.

get.description:
Report the REST/contract version and the package build version.

Both values come from ``gaia_agent_email.version`` — the same constants the
freeze server's root ``/version`` reads — so the product surface and the
frozen binary can never disagree on what contract they speak.
